proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_030 carries no information for objective_response because it is constant across patients.

Among the binary covariates, at least one feature has a large positive marginal association with objective_response (a candidate treatment or favourable biomarker), and several others have a large negative marginal association (candidate adverse prognostic factors).

Several binary covariates with moderate prevalence have null marginal associations with objective_response, consistent with randomized or quasi-randomized treatment assignment with no average effect, or with neutral demographic features.

analyses.result_summary: feature_030 is constant (single value 0 in all 50000 rows). Dropped from analysis.

analyses.code: for c in binary_feats: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Binary features with strong marginal associations: feature_008 (rate 36.1% vs 15.9%, RD=+0.202, p<1e-300), feature_013 (15.4% vs 34.6%, RD=-0.192, p<1e-300), feature_015 (16.0% vs 26.0%, RD=-0.101, p=2e-98), feature_021 (15.0% vs 25.0%, RD=-0.101, p=5e-56), feature_027 (17.6% vs 24.2%, RD=-0.066, p=3e-9). Null-marginal binaries: feature_005, 006, 004, 011, 017, 019, 023 all have RD ~ 0 and p > 0.3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature_001 (levels 0,1,2) is monotonically and inversely associated with objective_response: response rate decreases as feature_001 increases.
The ordinal feature_010 (Gleason-like, levels 6-10) is NOT associated with objective_response on average.
The continuous feature_022 (heavily right-skewed, range 0.1-3622, PSA-like) is inversely associated with objective_response.
Among the remaining continuous laboratory-like features, feature_020 (range 0-24.6) is inversely and feature_002 (range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_001 level: 0->28.2%, 1->22.8%, 2->18.2%. Logistic trend coefficient = -0.285, p=9.5e-73. Strong monotone inverse trend confirmed.
Response rates 24.4%, 23.7%, 24.2%, 24.2%, 24.2% across levels 6-10. Trend coefficient 0.005, p=0.61. No marginal association.

analyses.code: for c in cont_feats: Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[c]))
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 beta=-0.0047, p=1.6e-94 (strong inverse). feature_020 beta=-0.031, p=3.4e-29 (inverse). feature_024 beta=-0.0069, p=5.6e-8 (inverse). feature_002 beta=+0.096, p=4.4e-6 (positive). All other continuous features non-significant after Bonferroni.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 acts like a treatment indicator: in the cohort overall, patients with feature_008=1 have substantially higher objective_response than those with feature_008=0 (response rate ~36% vs ~16%, OR ~3.0).

The treatment-like effect of feature_008 is heterogeneous: it interacts strongly with at least one other binary covariate. Specifically, feature_013 attenuates or eliminates the feature_008 effect (multiplicative interaction term significantly negative).

analyses.result_summary: Marginal: response rate 36.14% (feature_008=1, n=20076) vs 15.89% (feature_008=0, n=29924). RD=+0.202, OR=2.99, p<1e-300.

Interaction logistic: beta_t (feature_008) = +2.04, beta_int (feature_008*feature_013) = -1.98, p_interaction effectively 0. Stratified rates: in feature_013=0 (n=22519) feature_008 lifts response from 16.9% to 61.0% (RD=+0.440, OR=7.66, p=0); in feature_013=1 (n=27481) the effect collapses to RD=+0.008, OR=1.06, p=0.09.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 also attenuates the treatment-like effect of feature_008 (negative multiplicative interaction).
feature_021 also attenuates the treatment-like effect of feature_008 (negative multiplicative interaction).
feature_027 also attenuates the treatment-like effect of feature_008 (negative multiplicative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_008*feature_015 interaction: beta=-1.24, p=1.4e-93. Stratified: in feature_015=0 (n=39962) RD=+0.251 (OR=3.67, p=0); in feature_015=1 (n=10038) RD=+0.008 (OR=1.06, p=0.27).
feature_008*feature_021 interaction: beta=-1.14, p=3.3e-42. Stratified: in feature_021=0 (n=45004) RD=+0.225 (OR=3.28, p=0); in feature_021=1 (n=4996) RD=+0.006 (OR=1.05, p=0.57).
feature_008*feature_027 interaction: beta=-1.28, p=1.8e-19. Stratified: in feature_027=0 (n=48472) RD=+0.209 (OR=3.09, p=0); in feature_027=1 (n=1528) RD=-0.022 (OR=0.86, p=0.30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature_022 modifies the treatment-like effect of feature_008: the treatment effect is larger at lower values of feature_022 and shrinks toward zero at higher values.
The treatment-like effect of feature_008 is approximately constant across age (feature_016), Gleason-like grade (feature_010), and the ordinal feature_001.

analyses.code: stratify by qcut(feature_022, 4); compute RD of feature_008 in each quartile
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 quartile RDs of feature_008: Q1 (0.12-6.46) RD=+0.367; Q2 (6.47-15.86) RD=+0.260; Q3 (15.87-41.28) RD=+0.148; Q4 (41.29-3622) RD=+0.034. Strong monotone shrinkage. Multiplicative interaction in logistic: p=1.2e-72.

analyses.code: stratified RD of feature_008 across age tertiles, feature_010 levels, feature_001 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_008 RD essentially constant across age tertiles (0.20 in each), feature_010 (RDs 0.20,0.20,0.20,0.20,0.23 across levels 6-10), and feature_001 (RDs 0.21, 0.20, 0.20 across levels 0,1,2). Logistic interactions of feature_008 with these in joint multivariable model are non-significant after adjustment for the four flags (p>0.2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four binary covariates feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 are mutually statistically INDEPENDENT — they are not redundant codings of the same underlying state.

The apparent modification of feature_008 by continuous feature_022 is fully mediated by feature_013 (i.e., feature_022 is correlated with feature_013 and contributes no independent modification once feature_013 is in the model).

analyses.result_summary: All six pairwise chi-square p-values are >0.37 and all phi correlations |phi|<0.005. The four flags are statistically independent of one another.

analyses.code: joint Logit with feature_008 plus interactions with feature_013, 015, 021, 027, 022, 020, 002, 001
analyses.result_summary: In a joint logistic model that simultaneously includes feature_008 interactions with all four flags AND with continuous feature_022, only the four flag interactions remain highly significant (p<1e-10 each); feature_008*feature_022 falls to p=0.54, feature_008*feature_020 p=0.58, feature_008*feature_002 p=0.59, feature_008*feature_001 p=0.22. Median feature_022 is 7.4 in feature_013=0 vs 32.9 in feature_013=1, confirming confounding. Inside the joint feature_013=15=21=27=0 stratum, the feature_008 RD is essentially constant across feature_022 quartiles (+0.633, +0.628, +0.624, +0.618).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup hypothesis: among patients with all four flags absent (feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0), feature_008 substantially raises objective_response (response rate >75% on treatment vs <20% off treatment).
Conversely, in any patient with at least one flag set, feature_008 has effectively no effect on objective_response (RD < 0.03).

analyses.result_summary: fav=1 (n=15681, 31.4% of cohort): off-treatment 17.21% (n=9356), on-treatment 79.78% (n=6325), RD=+62.6 percentage points. fav=0 (n=34319, 68.6%): off-treatment 15.30%, on-treatment 16.06%, RD=+0.77 pp. Logit with feature_008*fav interaction: beta_t (fav=0)=+0.058 (p=0.055), beta_t+beta_int (fav=1)=+2.94 (OR=18.98), interaction p < 1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_008 collapses essentially completely as soon as any one of the four flags is set; additional flags beyond the first do not further reduce the effect (i.e., the heterogeneity is governed by 'any flag' rather than a graded count).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified RD of feature_008 by number of flags: n_flags=0 RD=+0.626 (n=15681); n_flags=1 RD=+0.009 (n=25372); n_flags=2 RD=+0.008 (n=8186); n_flags=3 RD=-0.022 (n=745); n_flags=4 RD=-0.091 (n=16). The drop is essentially complete at the first flag — additional flags do not further attenuate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (all four flags absent), the feature_008 treatment effect is approximately constant across age (feature_016), Gleason-like grade (feature_010), feature_001, and feature_022 — i.e., the favorable predicate is a complete characterization of the responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: Within fav==1 stratum, RD of feature_008 by tertile/level of additional features
analyses.result_summary: Inside favorable subgroup: RD by feature_022 quartile: 0.633, 0.628, 0.624, 0.618 (essentially flat). RD by age tertile: 0.626, 0.619, 0.632. RD by feature_010: 0.629, 0.621, 0.635, 0.615, 0.634. RD by feature_001: 0.646, 0.622, 0.590 (small numerical decline; feature_008*feature_001 within fav: p=0.069, not significant after Bonferroni). All other continuous features show flat treatment effects within the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of the other null-marginal binary features (feature_005, feature_006, feature_004, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023) contains a hidden treatment-like effect that emerges in any subgroup defined by the four flags or by feature_008 status.

analyses.code: stratify by (fav, feature_008); rate of objective_response across each null-marginal binary
analyses.result_summary: Within fav=1 (n=15681), all null-marginal binaries have |RD| < 0.018 with p > 0.16. Within feature_008=0 (n=29924), all |RD| < 0.010. Within feature_008=1 (n=20076), all |RD| < 0.013. Pairwise null x null interaction screen: best p (feature_006 x feature_019) = 0.010, not significant after Bonferroni across 21 tests. Pairwise null x flag interaction screen: minimum p = 0.031, again not significant after Bonferroni. No hidden treatment-effect heterogeneity is detectable in these features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A shallow CART decision tree fit to predict objective_response will identify the same dominant split structure: feature_008 first, then flags feature_013, feature_015, feature_021 in the feature_008=1 branch.

analyses.result_summary: Tree's first split is feature_008 (<=0.5 vs >0.5). On the feature_008>0.5 branch, the next two splits are feature_013<=0.5 and feature_015<=0.5, then feature_021<=0.5. The only leaf with predicted class 1 is reached via feature_008=1 AND feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0; all other leaves predict class 0. This independently confirms the joint-flag subgroup definition derived from the regression interaction screen.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable logistic that includes feature_008, all four flags and their feature_008 interactions, plus continuous features, the four flag-by-feature_008 interaction terms remain large and significant while continuous-by-feature_008 interactions are not, confirming the four flags as the irreducible set of effect modifiers.

After adjustment, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 retain large independent negative main-effect contributions to log-odds of response (i.e., they are independent prognostic factors as well as effect modifiers).

analyses.code: Logit(y, [const, feature_008, flags, continuous, feature_008*flags, feature_008*continuous])
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_008-by-flag interaction coefficients: x feature_013 = -2.24 (p<1e-300), x feature_015 = -1.64 (p~5e-140), x feature_021 = -1.55 (p~9e-67), x feature_027 = -1.66 (p~2e-27). All four are large, negative, and independently significant. Continuous feature_008-by-feature_022, _020, _002, _001 interactions are all non-significant in the joint model (p>=0.22). The four flags are the irreducible set.

Full main-effects logistic adjusted main-effect coefficients (log odds): feature_008 +1.20, feature_013 -1.13, feature_015 -0.69, feature_021 -0.74, feature_027 -0.43, feature_001 -0.33, feature_022 -0.0010, feature_020 -0.036, feature_002 +0.13. All p<1e-8 except feature_027 p=2.5e-9. The four flags retain large, independent prognostic contributions after mutual adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis: feature_008 (the implied treatment) raises objective_response from approximately 17% to approximately 80% (absolute risk difference ~+62.6 percentage points, OR ~19) in the subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0; in any patient with at least one of these four flags set, feature_008 has no clinically meaningful effect (RD < 0.03, OR ~1.06). The four flags are mutually independent prognostic factors and each, on its own, fully suppresses the treatment effect.

analyses.code: rate by (favorable subgroup, feature_008); RD; OR via Logit
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (n=15681, 31.4% of cohort): off-treatment response 17.21%, on-treatment 79.78%, RD=+62.6 pp, OR=18.98 (95% CI from logit: ~17.1-21.1), p<1e-300. Non-favorable subgroup (n=34319): off-treatment 15.30%, on-treatment 16.06%, RD=+0.77 pp, OR=1.06, p=0.10. The favorable-subgroup-by-feature_008 interaction term is the largest single explanatory term in any model considered. This is the final best-supported treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for objective_response in this cohort.
